And besides that, at the same time, I am a freelancer for a Lithuanian company.
It is based in Lithuania.
It's a startup based in Lithuania.
But right now we have over 60 nationalities working together because it's an entertainment and media company.
And we produce videos for YouTube and Facebook.
So we have different teams of different languages for this.
And in this case, I am a translator.
I started as a translator translating the text for the videos.
Then I was promoted to a virtual assistant.

Because what I do is I create the voiceovers, the voices with a...
for the videos.
So it's the second step of the process.
The first is the text, then I generate this text with AI, 11 labs most of the time, and then it goes on to editing, and so on and so forth.

And, well, that's basically my job, but what I do mostly is try to come up with solutions to make the machine work, because, as I told you, in nationalities, everyone does things in their own pace, in their own way, and then when the file, the actual file, actually gets to me, for example, to generate the audio, because I work with Norwegian, Swedish, Turkish, Portuguese, and one more language I don't remember now.
So, it's different people in different languages doing different things.
So, I came up, first, I came up with a specific chatty PT box.
To put all the files in the same format so that it would make things easier for me.
That saved me around 10% of the actual turnaround time that I was spending during the task.
And after that, when I started working with more complex projects, because at first it was a mono voice one, so it was just one person speaking.
Now I am working with a project that has 10 characters, 15 characters, so it got more complex.
I started to notice something very, very, very complicated, that was the translators were not watching the video, and then the text was not following the video.
And since I don't actually speak Norwegian, I couldn't edit it, and we were...
And in a long time, actually, like I was editing it with Google Translator and ChatDBT, just because we didn't have the time to make it come back to the translator, so it would come back to me.
So I was doing this, which was not my job, and it was dangerous because I didn't speak the language.
Right, exactly.
Exactly.
So I was like, okay, I need to come up with a solution for this because I know translators.
I am a translator.
If I tell them to do something, they are not going to do.
I need to show them how you're going to do this.
And then, like, for me, the answer was transparency.
When I was just a translator, I didn't really understand the complete workflow and the complete process.
And I thought that by showing this to the translators, they would actually understand how their work impacted others.
And simple things they could do to...
So I created a style guide, we didn't have this in the beginning, with the format and the rules and the things you have to do, the workflow, etc.

And I proposed to the HR that I did a training session with the translators, so that I could actually show them live, me going to the 311 Labs, putting the text there, generating, for them to actually understand how it works.
Because AI is very new, and for translation, it's even newer.
So in my mind, it was like, okay, I think they don't actually imagine that this exists.
They don't know that this happens.
And then after that, that was some three or four weeks ago, after the training and after the style guide was developed, we have seen me and the other virtual assistants, there are two more.
I've seen that our work time has reduced by 50%, because 50% was just checking the actual text to say if it made sense, and now we don't do this anymore.
So everyone's lives is better now, and we have a style guide.
I've been working for them since 2023, and asking for a style guide since the beginning, never got it.
So I was like, okay, I'm going to make it, because nobody is actually doing it.
You've got to do it, yeah.
Exactly, somebody has got to do it.
